title: [POC] New URLSync mechanism
body: **Proof of concept** - not production ready. This PR proposes a new API for synchronizing the search parameters with the URL. Here are the key goals of this new implementation: - deeper customization of the URL - handling the no-value / default value case and do not render any crufts - make the state more accessible - possibility to handle storages that are not URL's First, what does not change with this new implementation: the lifecycle itself. The URL is stored when there is a new rendering and a new search is triggered when there is an update of the URL. This PoC is an effort to make the internals customizable. So anything that is not related to the lifecycle is externalized as API's. We will provide a default implementation that will work but may not be to the taste of everyone, hence the customization part. ## UI State One of the biggest change is that the widget can now provide UI-oriented views of the state. This is inspired by the state of react-instantsearch. This is this view that will be manipulated by the synchronization. Unlike react-instantsearch, this view is not the search parameters. If you want a widget to be synchronizable, it needs to implement two methods: - `getUIState`: this methods transforms the searchParameters into a view of the state of this widget and adds it in an object that is returned (API is like a reducer). The [implementation for the searchbox](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/c7867ff4e705e009b90b22f145a7aff97340c7b0/src/connectors/search-box/connectSearchBox.js#L155-L162) reads the searchParameters and adds a key `query` in the UI state object with the value of the current query. - `getSearchParameters`: this methods does the opposite, reads a UI state and update the search parameters accordingly. Here is an [example with the searchbox](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/c7867ff4e705e009b90b22f145a7aff97340c7b0/src/connectors/search-box/connectSearchBox.js#L164-L166) This PoC implements the [synchronization for the refinementList](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/c7867ff4e705e009b90b22f145a7aff97340c7b0/src/connectors/refinement-list/connectRefinementList.js#L413-L433) as well. ## Storage synchronization For the synchronization itself, there are two aspects: - the mapping - the storage This part has been split in two so that it's always possible to customization the structure without having to reimplement the storage which may be more tedious. The UI state mapping is an object with two methods: - `toSyncable` - `fromSyncable` The syncable object is the simplified structure that will be used directly by the storage. The storage shall not make any changes in this structure. We have currently a [single implementation](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/c7867ff4e705e009b90b22f145a7aff97340c7b0/src/lib/uiStateMapping/simple.js) that just transfers the UIState without any changes. The storage part is inspired by both the [work on react-instantsearch](https://github.com/algolia/react-instantsearch/issues/271) for an API to transfer the state of IS into another store and the experience that we gained with the URL sync. The storage API has 5 methods: - `write`: writes a syncable object to the storage - `read`: reads the storage and gets a syncable object - `onUpdate`: registers a callback that will be triggered when the storage is updated - `createURL`: creates a URL. May not be applicable. For now a default implemented must be provided. - `dispose`: unregister the storage adapter. Cleans up the event listeners. This PoC offers a [single implementation](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/c7867ff4e705e009b90b22f145a7aff97340c7b0/src/lib/storage/modern.js) that is based on the code from the current URL sync and it uses the [modern browser history API.](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/History) ## Overview ## Demos - [with default implementations based on modern history API](https://disastrous-approval.surge.sh) ([source](http://disastrous-approval.surge.sh/app.js)) - [With non-trivial mappings](http://weary-plants.surge.sh/Cameras%20&%20Camcorders/Binoculars?brand=Canon), brand refinement list data is mapped to brand query string parameter and the hierarchical menu is mapped to the path name of the URL - ([source](http://weary-plants.surge.sh/app.js)) - [start page count at 1 in the URL](http://nebulous-balls.surge.sh/?page=3) - ([Source](http://nebulous-balls.surge.sh/app.js)) ## Next steps - [x] Implement debouncing of the write. I'll try to implement this in the data storage in order to not clutter - [ ] Implement other widgets. I don't think this matter much in the context of this PoC unless you see some specific widget where it could be a problem. I'll have a look at react-instantsearch also. - [x] Try to implement a custom writer that will rewrite the path. What I have in mind is a hierarchical menu path in the URL and some custom query parameters.
